vocab.meaning: vật trang trí trung tâm/chủ đạo
vocab.example: I purchased a large sunburst mirror to serve as a decorative centerpiece in my otherwise plain living room.

vocab.meaning: sự hòa nhập/kết hợp mượt mà vào...
vocab.example: When selecting a mirror for the hallway, I looked for a frame that offered a seamless integration into the existing color palette.

vocab.meaning: tạo ra/mang lại cảm giác về chiều sâu
vocab.example: I strategically bought a floor-to-ceiling mirror to impart a sense of depth to my studio apartment.

vocab.meaning: góc nhìn thị giác bị biến dạng
vocab.example: I tend to avoid cheap, low-quality mirrors as they often result in a distorted visual perspective.

vocab.meaning: một công cụ hỗ trợ làm đẹp/chỉnh chu không thể thiếu
vocab.example: I invested in a high-magnification mirror which has become an indispensable grooming aid for my morning routine.
The bronze-tinted mirror I bought really helps to harmonize with the warm tones of the interior.

vocab.meaning: một món đồ cổ/đồ cũ có giá trị tìm thấy được
vocab.example: That baroque-style mirror was a lucky vintage find from a small local auction house.
Since I have young children, I made sure the last mirror I bought had shatter-resistant properties for safety.

vocab.meaning: làm nổi bật/nhấn mạnh chiều cao
vocab.example: By buying a long, slender mirror, I managed to accentuate the verticality of the room, making the ceiling appear higher.

vocab.meaning: đạt được sự đối xứng về mặt thị giác
vocab.example: I bought a pair of identical arched mirrors to place on either side of the fireplace to achieve visual symmetry.
